Yulang `v0.1.0-alpha.5` is an alpha release focused on making the evidence VM
the default runtime path and improving playground responsiveness.

## Highlights

- Switched the default CLI `run` path to the evidence VM while keeping the
  control VM available through explicit debug/control options.
- Switched the browser playground `run` path to the evidence VM as well, with
  the same output payload shape as before.
- Optimized the evidence VM handler path substantially, including native close
  branches for certified provider-permission boundaries and prefix boundaries.
- Preserved playground-style constructor and type display: canonical CLI/debug
  output remains stable, while playground output uses a context-aware shortest
  suffix display and ML-style constructor application where it is readable.
- Kept hosted playground/docs updated with the faster runtime route.
- Refreshed the Zed extension README installer guidance for this release; the
  extension package version remains `0.0.10`.

## Install

Unix-like systems:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://yulang.momota.pw/install.sh | sh -s -- --version v0.1.0-alpha.5
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Invoke-WebRequest https://yulang.momota.pw/install.ps1 -OutFile install.ps1
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\install.ps1 -Version v0.1.0-alpha.5
```

## Known Limitations

- The language and standard library remain alpha-stage and may still change.
- Local realm/band support is still limited to local editable and installed
  snapshots. Remote providers, lock-file UX, and version-family solving remain
  future work.
- The evidence VM is now the default, but the control VM remains available as a
  fallback/debug path while the runtime transition continues.
- Playground display shortening is currently based on compiled-program path
  uniqueness; a future step can make it share the exact module-scope display
  context used by the language server.
